page: Amazon Web Services i4i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs, which correspond to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The CPU includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8 GB of RAM per physical core.

A key feature of the i4i.8xlarge is its bundled local storage, consisting of 7500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, designed for high-throughput and low-latency data access. The server operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance. The combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and dedicated memory provides a resource-dense configuration, offering qualitative cost efficiency for applications that would otherwise require expensive external storage attachments.

Benchmark results place the i4i.8xlarge consistently in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. It achieved a Passmark CPU score of 36608.74 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13416.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1658.71 GB/sec, while large block uncached read and write speeds reached 125.36 GB/sec and 62.92 GB/sec respectively. For database workloads, the server demonstrated 31.16 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a Passmark database score of 14843.76.

In language model inference tasks, the server shows strong performance in text generation for small 135M models at 301.68 tokens per second, but drops to a weak tier of 6.2 tokens per second for prompt processing on large 70B models. Given its massive local NVMe storage, high memory capacity, and balanced compute profile, the i4i.8xlarge is suited for transactional databases, NoSQL databases, data warehousing, and real-time analytics workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server designed for data-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.5 GHz, it features 256 GB of DDR4 memory and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate solid mid-tier performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 36608.74 and a Redis SET throughput of 31.16 million operations per second. The server exhibits strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, though large-model processing is limited. This hardware profile makes the i4i.8xlarge suitable for transactional databases, data warehousing, and analytical applications requiring low-latency local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering reliable mid-tier database and analytical performance.

tagline: A storage-optimized server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors, 256 GB RAM, and 7.5 TB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.

7500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for transactional databases, NoSQL, and data warehousing
